content:
# Hoffuf Formation

**Summary**: A Miocene formation characterized by a variety of lithologies including conglomerate, sandstone, and mudstone.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] investigated the Hofuf Formation to understand its structural features and role in the aquifer system.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] not stated in source

## Reservoir Properties

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] thickness 93m at Hofuf city

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Hofuf Formation varies in thickness (up to 93m) and contains a base of white/brown conglomerate followed by calcareous sandstone and red mudstone. ([[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024, p. 36]])

## Conflicting Interpretations Across Sources

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] not stated in source
